vision_api_reliability: Labels and faces are accurate. Text detection picks up background text (books, manuscripts). Word count metric is noisy.
analysis_method: Google Cloud Vision API: LABEL_DETECTION + TEXT_DETECTION + IMAGE_PROPERTIES + FACE_DETECTION

text_presence.insight: Nearly universal. Text presence alone is not a differentiator.

faces_overall.insight: Faces are NEUTRAL overall (49% vs 51%). The 'faces bad' finding was cross-channel noise.
faces_overall.per_channel: Varies by channel. ESOTERICA faces neutral (19% vs 18%). Philosophy Tube faces positive. Check per-channel data.

colors.insight: Breakout thumbnails are slightly lighter. Not a strong signal.

text_word_count_caveat.warning: Word count includes background text from books and manuscripts. The meaningful signal is DESIGNED text overlay (1-5 words) vs BACKGROUND text (books, documents).

strongest_signal.source: ESOTERICA within-channel analysis (controls for title format)
strongest_signal.finding: Breakout thumbnails have ~6 designed words vs 21 background-inflated words for normal. Designed 1-3 word overlay outperforms no text or cluttered background text.

best_practices.thumbnail_style: Designed graphic composition (not photo of person/object)
best_practices.thumbnail_text: 1-3 punchy designed words. Never rely on text from a book/page photo.
best_practices.faces: Neutral — don't avoid, don't force. Let the topic dictate.
best_practices.colors: Dark theme works but add contrast. Avoid all-dark with no highlights.
best_practices.title_style: Question format ('What is X?') or 'The [Secret/Ancient] Of [Topic]'
